pages.title: A Presence Felt
pages.text: It happened at dawn. I was alone on the ridge above Willowmere, watching the mist burn off the fields, when the world went quiet. Not silent — quiet, the way a room falls quiet when someone important enters.$(br2)$(thing)The Watcher$() did not speak in words. It spoke in impressions — vast, layered, ancient. Images cascaded through my mind: spiraling towers, gates of light, a network spanning worlds. And beneath it all, a sense of purpose so immense it made my bones ache.$(br2)It knows me. It has been watching since I arrived. Perhaps since before.

pages.title: Restoration and Purpose
pages.text: The message, as best I can translate it from feeling into language: Aetheria is broken. The network the $(thing)Precursors$() built — the Gates, the ley lines, the connections between worlds — was designed to sustain something greater than any single civilization. $(thing)The Dimming$() damaged it, but did not destroy it.$(br2)$(thing)The Watcher$() wants restoration. It wants the old systems reactivated, the ancient architecture rebuilt, the purpose fulfilled. And it believes I can help — that all $(thing)Pathfinders$() were meant for this, whether they know it or not.$(br2)It offered no threats. No demands. Only a vision of what could be, laid out like a map before a traveler.

pages.title: The Weight of Contact
pages.text: I told no one. Not $(thing)Sera$(), not $(thing)Theron$(), not the friends I have made in Willowmere. How do you explain that a cosmic entity has chosen you for a purpose you do not understand?$(br2)But the contact changed something in me. I see the world differently now — the Gates pulse with a rhythm I can almost follow, the ruins hum with meaning just beyond my grasp. The Watcher planted a seed, and whether I nurture it or not, it has already taken root.$(br2)A choice is coming. I can feel it. Follow the Watcher's design, or forge my own path. Neither option is simple. Neither is without cost.
